q0101.help: Indicate the number of member and/or collaborator persons of the organization who are NOT salaried (e.g., consumer members, service members, or professional members in the case of second-degree or worker cooperatives)[cite: 1].

q0102.help: Only for second and third-degree organizations. Indicate the number of companies or entities that are members[cite: 1].

q0103.text: Persons participating on behalf of partner/patron organizations
q0103.help: Only for second and third-degree entities. Indicate the number of representatives from partner organizations who actively participate in decision-making and executive spaces[cite: 1].

q0104.help: Only for foundations. Indicate the number of persons who make up the foundation's board of trustees[cite: 1].

q0105.help: Indicate the number of working persons who are, at the same time, members of the organization[cite: 1].

q0106.help: Indicate the number of salaried employees who are not members of the organization[cite: 1].

q0107.help: For all entities that regularly rely on the presence of volunteers due to the nature of their activity. Indicate the number of people who voluntarily participate in the association's structure[cite: 1].

q0110.text: Number of working persons who are not on the payroll
q0110.help: Indicate the number of working persons who DO NOT have an employment contract linked to the project, segregated by sex (e.g., freelance workers or professionals who submit a monthly or quarterly invoice/receipt)[cite: 1].

q0112.help: Indicate the number of communal land partners. Only for communal neighborhood forests ('montes vecinales en mano común')[cite: 1].

q0201.text: Number of workplaces in the organization
q0201.help: Indicate the total quantity of workplaces (offices, workshops, factories, etc.) of the organization[cite: 1].

q1101.text: Total number of jobs expressed in full-time equivalent (FTE) days worked
q1101.help: Report the full-time equivalent days worked by all persons who had an employment contract during the year. Include cooperative working members and freelancers with exclusive dedication (counted as 1 FTE)[cite: 1].

q1216.text: Indicate the Social Security regime for cooperative working members
q1216.help: Specify if workers are registered under the General Regime or the Special Regime for Self-Employed Workers (RETA)[cite: 1].

q1102.text: Sum of the annual gross salary of all working persons of the entity in the last fiscal year
q1102.help: Total sum of gross annual salaries of all workers without including indemnities. This can be found under account 640 of the General Accounting Plan[cite: 1].

q1107.text: Sum of remunerations not paid by the company/entity due to medical leave or permission
q1107.help: Total gross remunerations saved by the entity because they were assumed directly by the Social Security system during temporary leaves[cite: 1].

q1201.text: Total annual amount of expenses - in € -
q1201.help: Indicate the total annual expenses (sum of all items in group 6 'Purchases and Expenses' of the General Accounting Plan, including salaries, rent, purchases, etc.)[cite: 1].

q1203.text: Total annual amount of revenue/income - in € -
q1203.help: Indicate the total income from sales of goods/services, subsidies, donations, membership fees, etc. (Group 7 'Sales and Income' of the PGC)[cite: 1].

q1202.text: Total annual amount of acquisitions of goods and services - in € -
q1202.help: Total purchases made for merchandise (office supplies, industrial provisions, IT equipment) and contracted external services. Accounts 60 and 62 of the PGC[cite: 1].

q1206.text: Total annual amount of personnel expenses - in € -
q1206.help: Annual total personnel cost obtained from account 64 of the General Accounting Plan[cite: 1].

q1204.text: Total annual amount of sales of goods and services - in € -
q1204.help: Income perceived from sales or provisions of services (account 70 of the PGC), including invoicing to Public Administrations[cite: 1].

q1205.text: Total annual amount of subsidies granted to the organization - in € -
q1205.help: Sum of those amounts appearing in account 740 of the Profit and Loss statement. Exclude income from public service provisions with standard invoices[cite: 1].

q1405.text: Amount of economic surpluses distributed to partner companies or member persons
q1405.help: Specify the exact amount of benefits or surpluses distributed internally to the members or shareholders, if any[cite: 1].

q1406.text: Amount of economic surpluses dedicated to loss compensation
q1406.help: Amount of current surpluses used to clear or compensate financial losses carried forward from previous years[cite: 1].

q1407.text: Amount of economic surpluses dedicated to reserves: Education / internal training
q1407.help: Surpluses allocated directly to internal educational, cooperative promotion, or technical training funds[cite: 1].

q1408.text: Amount of economic surpluses dedicated to reserves: Social and solidary causes
q1408.help: Surpluses allocated to donations or structural projects supporting external social and solidary movements[cite: 1].

q1409.text: Amount of economic surpluses dedicated to reserves: Financial investments in Social Economy financial entities
q1409.help: Surpluses dedicated to funding or increasing capital in entities like Coop57, Fiare, and/or Triodos Bank[cite: 1].

q1410.text: Amount of economic surpluses dedicated to reserves: Mandatory, voluntary, and other internal reserves
q1410.help: Surpluses assigned to general mandatory legal reserves, statutory voluntary reserves, etc[cite: 1].

q1411.text: Amount of economic surpluses dedicated to reserves: Others
q1411.help: Any other allocation of economic surpluses to internal funds not covered by previous categories[cite: 1].

q2101.text: Number of persons who participated in the elaboration of the Management Plan and annual budget
q2101.help: Total number of people involved in drafting the plan and budget. Verified via meeting minutes or internal draft distribution lists[cite: 1].

q2102.text: Number of persons who participated in the approval of the Management Plan and annual budget
q2102.help: Total number of people present or voting in the meetings where the management plans and annual budgets were formally approved[cite: 1].

q2110.text: Number of working persons who participated in the approval of the Management Plan and annual budget
q2110.help: Total number of internal workers or salaried employees who took part in the approval of the management plan and budget[cite: 1].

q2111.text: Number of working member persons who participated in the approval of the Management Plan and annual budget
q2111.help: Only for cooperatives. Total number of cooperative working members who participated in approving the annual plan and budget[cite: 1].

q3201.text: Number of persons occupying positions of responsibility in the labor structure of the organization
q3201.help: Number of people in management and middle-management roles within the labor structure, regardless of political/societary roles[cite: 1].

q3202.text: Number of persons occupying corporate/political positions in the organization
q3202.help: Number of members in governing bodies: board of directors (associations), governing council (cooperatives), trustees (foundations), or management council (social enterprises)[cite: 1].

q3301.text: Highest remuneration (in gross price per hour worked) - in € -
q3301.help: Highest gross hourly wage. Include gross salary plus representation fees or external meeting stipends paid due to holding a role[cite: 1].

q3302.text: Lowest remuneration (in gross price per hour worked) - in € -
q3302.help: Lowest gross hourly wage calculated by dividing the annual gross salary by the contract hours. Exclude interns or trainees[cite: 1].

q2202.text: Are salaries and other remunerations public for the working persons?
q2202.help: Indicate if there is an internal communication system (intranet, member area, internal email) where any member can access salary details[cite: 1].

q2203.text: Have we published the data/results of the Social Audit of the last fiscal year on the web and/or social networks?
q2203.help: Indicate whether the results of the previous balance sheet or social audit have been published openly on the organization's website[cite: 1].

q14401.text: Do you publish detailed organizational or environmental metrics openly via electronic channels?
q14401.help: Select if the entity provides structured operational and transparency disclosures on its digital platforms[cite: 1].

q3401.text: Does the entity use non-sexist and inclusive language?
q3401.help: Refers to the conscious adoption of language strategies that include both women and men equally (e.g., 'working staff' instead of masculine defaults)[cite: 1].

q3402.text: Does the entity have a valid equality plan and the resources to carry out the corresponding monitoring and evaluation?
q3402.help: Indicate whether a formal equality plan exists. Small entities (<5 workers) can validate this if daily informal practices active support equality[cite: 1].

q3403.text: Does the entity have a protocol for the prevention and addressing of sexual harassment, based on sex, gender identity, or sexual preference?
q3403.help: Indicate whether a formal harassment prevention protocol exists. Small entities (<5 workers) can validate this if daily practices informally handle it[cite: 1].

q3109.text: Average age of the working persons
q3109.help: The average age obtained by summing the age of all working persons and dividing by the total number of workers[cite: 1].

q3111.text: Have positive actions been applied to promote diversity in the entity?
q3111.help: Indicate whether specific active measures or campaigns have been introduced to foster inclusion and respect for diversity within the project[cite: 1].

q3108.text: Number of working persons with functional diversity / disability
q3108.help: Number of workers identified by the entity as having functional diversity or disability, regardless of official recognition by Social Security[cite: 1].

q5104.text: Is functional diversity taken into account and is the inclusion of all people promoted and/or facilitated?
q5104.help: Refers to spatial accessibility, adapted training materials, web accessibility compliance, or sign language support[cite: 1].

q4101.text: Do you have an environmental policy, action plan or management system?
q4101.help: Formalized tools (ISO 14000, EMAS) or own systems with published objectives to minimize environmental footprint. Small entities can validate informal practices[cite: 1].

q4102.text: Is an internal control of CO2 emissions carried out?
q4102.help: Refers to tracking resource consumptions (electricity, gas, mileage) and applying documented reduction or prevention measures[cite: 1].

q4201.text: Do you have practices and procedures for energy saving and efficiency?
q4201.help: Active measures favoring energy reduction. Ideally supported by registration sheets tracking investments and savings[cite: 1].

q4206.text: Do you have practices and procedures for saving and efficiency in water consumption?
q4206.help: Indicate whether the organization carries out active water-saving measures[cite: 1].

q4208.text: Do you contract all electrical services from supplier companies that generate 100% of their electrical energy from certified renewable sources with a guarantee of origin?
q4208.help: Accredited via Guarantees of Origin (GdO) erogated by CNMC. Partial contracting only applies to organizations with multiple workplaces[cite: 1].

q4301.text: Do you use recycled paper and/or paper with sustainable forest management certification on a regular and majority basis?
q4301.help: Accepted standard for white paper is FSC; PEFC is excluded due to active institutional controversies[cite: 1].

q4401.text: Do you have practices and procedures for waste prevention?
q4401.help: Measures targeting waste reduction, including criteria applied to purchases (avoiding single-use containers, selecting recyclable materials)[cite: 1].

q4503.text: When purchasing a product, are responsible consumption criteria taken into account?
q4503.help: Refers to purchasing items (food, supplies, furniture) with ecological manufacturing, local sourcing, Fair Trade, and/or second-hand criteria[cite: 1].

q4601.text: Does the entity incorporate, offer, or facilitate any alternative/help related to transport?
q4601.help: Includes carpooling, public transport financing, bicycle facilities, or teleworking setups to optimize internal mobility[cite: 1].

q50b.text: Do you actively participate in stable networks, federations, or social transformation initiatives?
q50b.help: Active participation requires a minimum of 30 hours per year and/or the payment of a regular membership fee[cite: 1].

q5202.text: Is the entity a partner or client of ethical and solidary finance institutions?
q5202.help: Indicate if the organization operates with entities like Coop57, Fiare, Oikocredit, or Triodos Bank[cite: 1].

q5302.text: Amount of purchases of goods and services from Social Market and/or REAS entities
q5302.help: Economic volume spent on purchases from providers within certified solidary networks (mercadosocial.net / economiasolidaria.org)[cite: 1].

q5305.text: Does the organization cooperate with others that provide the same products or services?
q5305.help: Measures sectorial cooperation over pure market competition. Must be documented via minutes, shared projects, or tenancy agreements[cite: 1].

q5306.text: Amount of purchases of goods and services made from Social Economy entities (NOT belonging to the Social Market or REAS)
q5306.help: Acquisitions from non-profit entities, associations, foundations, or insertion enterprises explicitly forbidding profit distribution[cite: 1].

q5501.text: Does your activity generate any type of good, service, or material made freely available to the commons or under free licenses like Creative Commons?
q5501.help: Sharing recipes, techniques, working tools, seeds, or creative knowledge so third parties can use them freely[cite: 1].

q5502.text: Do you implement explicit policies or commitments favoring the use of Free Software?
q5502.help: Utilization of open-source applications and tools ensuring user digital privacy and collective technological freedom[cite: 1].

q6202.text: Do you take active preventive and/or health promotion measures in your workplace?
q6202.help: Includes prevention training (avoiding risk control issues) and proactive promotion (fostering healthy diets, physical activity, tobacco cessation)[cite: 1].

q6601.text: Does the organization have an internal regulation or protocol that includes measures to improve the working conditions established in the applicable collective agreement?
q6601.help: Requires an internal framework officially improving sector standards (e.g., better leave policies, paid vacations, higher sick coverage)[cite: 1].

q6701.text: Do you apply specific measures to promote the professional and technical training of workers?
q6701.help: Refers to structural policies enhancing professional capacity in skills and scopes proper to the organization's mission[cite: 1].

q6801.text: Are measures that improve legal permits for balancing family, personal, and work life contemplated and formalized in writing?
q6801.help: Formal internal frameworks improving legal balance parameters (e.g., longer parental or care leave). Small entities can validate active informal practices[cite: 1].

q6802.text: Are emotional support and care spaces generated for workers in relation to their situation with the work environment?
q6802.help: Regular systems to share personal feelings and feedback regarding the work space (emotional check-ins, care commissions, etc.)[cite: 1].
